name: Baby Goat Simmered with Almonds and Saffron
recipeIngredient: 2-3 ounces Pancetta, diced into 1 1/2-inch chunks (trimmed of any membrane or fat)
4 medium Russet potatoes, peeled and cut into eighths
recipeInstructions:
1. In a non-stick skillet, heat butter and sauté almonds until lightly browned. Transfer to a food processor with saffron and process until finely ground. Set aside.
2. Add Pancetta to the same skillet and sauté until lightly cooked. Brown Baby goat meat in 2 batches and add to the pancetta.
3. Sauté onion until golden, adding more butter if needed. Stir in garlic, tomatoes, almond-saffron mixture, thyme, and bay leaf.
4. Season with salt and pepper. Add enough water to barely cover the ingredients. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and partially cover the pan.
5. Simmer for 1 hour or until meat is almost done. Add potatoes, pushing them under the meat, and cook for an additional 15 minutes or until tender (add a little more water if too dry).
6. Taste seasoning before serving.
Serves: 4
Nutritional Information: 545 calories; protein 34 g; carbohydrate 53 g; fat 24 g (7 g saturated); cholesterol 82 mg; sodium 238 mg; fiber 5 g
